fields.name: Code
fields.tooltip: Specifies a code that identifies the depreciation book.

fields.name: Description
fields.tooltip: Specifies the purpose of the depreciation book.

fields.name: G/L Integration - Acq. Cost
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether acquisition cost entries posted to this depreciation book are posted both to the general ledger and the FA ledger.

fields.name: G/L Integration - Depreciation
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether depreciation entries posted to this depreciation book are posted both to the general ledger and the FA ledger.

fields.name: G/L Integration - Write-Down
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether write-down entries posted to this depreciation book should be posted to the general ledger and the FA ledger.

fields.name: G/L Integration - Appreciation
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether appreciation entries posted to this depreciation book are posted to the general ledger and the FA ledger.

fields.name: G/L Integration - Custom 1
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether custom 1 entries posted to this depreciation book are posted to the general ledger and the FA ledger.

fields.name: G/L Integration - Custom 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether custom 2 entries posted to this depreciation book are posted to the general ledger and the FA ledger.

fields.name: G/L Integration - Disposal
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether disposal entries posted to this depreciation book are posted to the general ledger and the FA ledger.

fields.name: G/L Integration - Maintenance
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether maintenance entries that are posted to this depreciation book are posted both to the general ledger and the FA ledger.

fields.name: Disposal Calculation Method
fields.tooltip: Specifies the disposal method for the current depreciation book.

fields.name: Use Custom 1 Depreciation
fields.tooltip:
Specifies if you want to use depreciation of Custom 1 entries.
Then, in the FA depreciation book or books, you can define the way custom 1 depreciation must be handled by the program.

fields.name: Allow Depr. below Zero
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether to allow the Calculate Depreciation batch job to continue calculating depreciation even if the book value is zero or below.

fields.name: Use FA Exch. Rate in Duplic.
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether to use the FA Exchange Rate field when you duplicate entries from one depreciation book to another.

fields.name: Part of Duplication List
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether to indicate that entries made in another depreciation book should be duplicated to this depreciation book.

fields.name: G/L Integration - Bonus Depr.
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether bonus depreciation entries that are posted to this depreciation book are posted both to the general ledger and the FA ledger.

fields.name: Last Date Modified
fields.tooltip:
Shows when the depreciation book was last modified.
When you change information on the depreciation book card, the program automatically updates the Last Date Modified field to show the current system date.

fields.name: Allow Indexation
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether to allow indexation of FA ledger entries and maintenance ledger entries posted to this book.

fields.name: Use Same FA+G/L Posting Dates
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether to indicate that the Posting Date and the FA Posting Date must be the same on a journal line before posting.

fields.name: Default Exchange Rate
fields.tooltip: Specifies the exchange rate to use if the rate in the FA Exchange Rate field is zero.

fields.name: Use FA Ledger Check
fields.tooltip: Specifies which checks to perform before posting a journal line.

fields.name: Use Rounding in Periodic Depr.
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the calculated periodic depreciation amounts should be rounded to whole numbers.

fields.name: New Fiscal Year Starting Date
fields.tooltip:
Specifies a date to specify the first date in the current fiscal year. If you leave the field blank, the program will use the date in the Starting Date field in the Accounting Periods window.
The information in this field is only used when the program calculates depreciation for fixed assets where the Depreciation Method is Declining Balance 1 or DB1/SL.
In this case the depreciation basis is calculated as book value minus depreciation in the fiscal year. Depreciation is defined in the field Depreciation Type in the FA Posting Type Setup window. The starting date of a new fiscal year is used to calculate depreciation in that fiscal year.

fields.name: No. of Days in Fiscal Year
fields.tooltip:
Contains the number of days in the fiscal year. If the field is blank, the program uses 360 days.
The first year a company uses the Depreciation Book the fiscal year might not be exactly 12 months. The value in the field must always, however, be a multiple of 30 (1 month).

fields.name: Allow Changes in Depr. Fields
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether to allow the depreciation fields to be modified.

fields.name: Default Final Rounding Amount
fields.tooltip: Specifies the final rounding amount to use if the Final Rounding Amount field is zero.

fields.name: Default Ending Book Value
fields.tooltip: Specifies the ending book value to use if the Ending Book Value field is zero.

fields.name: Periodic Depr. Date Calc.
fields.tooltip:
Specifies the appropriate option. The options are: Last Entry and Last Depr. Entry.
Last Entry
With this option, the program calculates the number of depreciation days as of the date of the last FA ledger entry.
Last Depr. Entry
With this option the program calculates the number of depreciation days as of the date of the last depreciation FA ledger entry (that is, the last entry where the posting type is Depreciation).

fields.name: Mark Errors as Corrections
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether to mark ledger entries as corrections if they are copied to an FA general ledger journal or an FA journal when you use the Cancel Entries function from the FA Ledger Entries window or run the Cancel FA Ledger Entries batch job from the depreciation book card. Then the program will place a check mark in the Correction field on the journal line(s).

fields.name: Add-Curr Exch Rate - Acq. Cost
fields.tooltip: Specifies that acquisition transactions in the general ledger can be in both LCY and any additional reporting currency.

fields.name: Add.-Curr. Exch. Rate - Depr.
fields.tooltip: Specifies depreciation transactions in the general ledger in both LCY and any additional reporting currency.

fields.name: Add-Curr Exch Rate -Write-Down
fields.tooltip: Specifies write-down transactions in the general ledger in both LCY and any additional reporting currency.

fields.name: Add-Curr. Exch. Rate - Apprec.
fields.tooltip: Specifies appreciation transactions in the general ledger in both LCY and any additional reporting currency.

fields.name: Add-Curr. Exch Rate - Custom 1
fields.tooltip: Specifies that custom 1 transactions in the general ledger can be in both LCY and any additional reporting currency.

fields.name: Add-Curr. Exch Rate - Custom 2
fields.tooltip: Specifies custom 2 transactions in the general ledger in both LCY and any additional reporting currency.

fields.name: Add.-Curr. Exch. Rate - Disp.
fields.tooltip: Specifies disposal transactions in the general ledger in both LCY and any additional reporting currency.

fields.name: Add.-Curr. Exch. Rate - Maint.
fields.tooltip: Specifies maintenance transactions in the general ledger in both LCY and any additional reporting currency.

fields.name: Use Default Dimension
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether to only copy the fixed asset default dimensions to the journal lines created for the duplicate depreciation book. If the field is empty, all dimensions from the journal lines being duplicated will be copied.

fields.name: Subtract Disc. in Purch. Inv.
fields.tooltip: Specifies that the line and invoice discount are subtracted from the acquisition cost posted for the fixed asset.

fields.name: Allow Correction of Disposal
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether to correct fixed ledger entries of the Disposal type.

fields.name: Allow more than 360/365 Days
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the fiscal year has more than 360 depreciation days.

fields.name: VAT on Net Disposal Entries
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether you sell a fixed asset with the net disposal method.

fields.name: Allow Acq. Cost below Zero
fields.tooltip: Specifies that you can index the acquisition cost of the fixed asset, even if the book value is below zero and you have selected the Allow Depr. below Zero field.

fields.name: Allow Identical Document No.
fields.tooltip: Specifies the check box for this field to allow identical document numbers in the depreciation book.

fields.name: Fiscal Year 365 Days
fields.tooltip: Specifies that when the Calculate Depreciation batch job calculates depreciations, a standardized year of 360 days, where each month has 30 days, is used.

fields.name: Use Bonus Depreciation
fields.tooltip: Specifies if the bonus depreciation should be used in this book.

fields.name: Use Accounting Period
fields.tooltip: Specifies if you want the periods between start date and ending date to correspond to the accounting periods that you have set up.
